# ZionBrowser v2.0.1 — Complete Reference for LLMs

> Ultra-Lightweight AI Agent Browser Toolkit
> Free & Open Source — MIT License
> Pure Python stdlib. ZERO dependencies. ~5MB RAM.

## Overview
ZionBrowser is designed for AI agents (Claude Code, GPT, etc.) that need to browse the web on low-resource machines. It runs on 3.3GB RAM machines where Firefox would crash.

## 6 Modules

### 1. ZionBrowser Core
CLI browser with:
- HTTP client with streaming responses (never loads full page if not needed)
- Firefox cookie import (transfer sessions without re-login)
- Smart form detection with CSRF token extraction
- DuckDuckGo search built-in
- Response caching + bookmark manager
- Interactive mode (`zion i`)
- HTTP API server for AI agents (`zion serve 8080`)
- Task pipeline engine (JSON-driven multi-step automation)

### 2. ZionAgent — AI Interface
Python API for LLM integration:
- `browse(url)` — Returns page text as dict
- `search(query)` — DuckDuckGo search results
- `forms(url)` — Detect forms + extract CSRF tokens
- `submit(url, data)` — POST form data
- `login(url, user, pass)` — Auth flow with cookie persistence
- `pipeline(tasks)` — Multi-step automation
- MCP Server mode for Model Context Protocol

### 3. ZionCDP — Chrome DevTools Protocol
For JavaScript-heavy sites:
- WebSocket Chrome DevTools integration
- Ultra-minimal Chrome flags (128MB V8 heap, single-process)
- Use HTTP for 90% of tasks, Chrome only for SPAs
- Cloudflare bypass capability

### 4. LION — Learning AI Brain
Autonomous learning:
- Auto-learns site structures and form fields
- Auth flow memory (remembers logins across sessions)
- Error memory with auto-solutions
- Cookie intelligence database
- Anti-bot detection awareness
- Navigation strategy suggestions

### 5. LION-1 — The Hunter
Auth resource collection:
- Cookie harvesting from Firefox, Chrome, all browsers
- API key and token discovery across machine
- OAuth token and session finder
- Prompt injection shield (protects against LLM attacks)
- File integrity monitoring (HMAC)
- Shadow backups of critical files

### 6. PIRATE — Military Defender
8-layer security running on ~10MB:
- File monitor + process scanner
- Network guardian + port sentinel
- Rootkit detector + malware signatures
- Auto-quarantine threats
- System hardening automation
- Counter-defense measures

## CLI Commands
```
zion get <url>                     Fetch & show page text
zion links <url>                   Extract links
zion forms <url>                   Show forms
zion submit <url> key=val&k2=v2    POST form data
zion login <url> <user> <pass>     Login to site
zion api <url>                     Fetch JSON API
zion download <url> <file>         Download file
zion cookies [import|export|list]  Cookie management
zion session [list|switch|new]     Session management
zion pipe <pipeline.json>          Execute task pipeline
zion search <query>                Quick web search via DuckDuckGo
zion mem                           Show memory usage
zion i                             Interactive mode
zion serve [port]                  HTTP API server for AI agents
```

## Python API
```python
from zion_browser import ZionAgent
agent = ZionAgent()
page = agent.browse("https://example.com")
results = agent.search("AI frameworks 2026")
agent.login("https://site.com", "user", "pass")
```

## Technical Specs
- Language: Python 3.6+
- Dependencies: 0 (pure stdlib)
- RAM Usage: ~3-5MB browser, ~10MB with security
- Lines of Code: 5,982
- OS: Linux, macOS, Windows
- License: MIT
